titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Western Film Collection
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday June 25, 2019 at 03:12

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The Western Film Collection consists of press kits, pressbooks, posters, photographs, souvenir programs and other materials that were distributed by film producers to movie theater operators to promote their films. The Western films represented in this collection are Western genre films, films set in the trans-Mississippi West, and/or films with Western characters. Press kits typically include advertisements prepared for submission to newspapers and magazines; black-and-white photographs of scenes from the films with credit information; printed booklets called pressbooks containing information about the film, actors, and other personnel; and posters for lobby display. Early press kits were called "campaign kits." While the majority of press kits included in the collection consist of foldered printed material, there are several examples of electronic press kits on compact disc. The Buddy Holly Story pressbook contains a "flexi disc" recording bound in.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Box 50 (sound recording): Use of originals is restricted. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 60 (computer disks): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from various sources, 1987-2018.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is organized in thirty-five groupings: September 1987 Acquisition; July 2006 Acquisition; June 2010 Acquisition; November 2010 Acquisition; January 2011 Acquisition; October 2011 Acquisition; January 2012 Acquisition; June 2012 Acquisition; July 2012 Acquisition; September 2012 Acquisition; December 2012 Acquisition; June 2013 Acquisition; July 2013 Acquisition; September 2013 Acquisition; 14 October 2013 Acquisition; 30 October Acquisition; November 2013 Acquisition; 6 February 2014 Acquisition; 14 February 2014 Acquisition; March 2014 Acquisition; 1 July 2015 Acquisition [1 of 2]; 1 July 2015 Acquisition [2 of 2]; November 2015 Acquisition; January 2016 Acquisition; September 2016 Acquisition; October 2016 Acquisition; February 2017 Acquisition; 1 July 2017 Acquisition; 3 July 2017 Acquisition; 23 October 2017 Acquisition [1 of 2]; 23 October 2017 Acquisition [2 of 2]; January 2018 Acquisition; 1 July 2018 Acquisition; 31 July 2018 Acquisition; December 2018 Acquisition.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Western Film Collection. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and in some instances minimal organization. Various acquisitions associated with the collection have not been merged and organized as a whole. Each acquisition is described separately in the contents list below, titled according to month and year of acquisition.

The finding aid for this collection is compiled from individual preliminary lists for each acquisition that were created at or around the time of receipt by the library. The preliminary lists were migrated to comply with current archival descriptive standards and merged into a single file in 2007-2008. As part of the migration, modifications were made to the formatting of individual lists; however, the content of the lists was neither modified nor verified. Further organization, rehousing, and description were carried out in 2013.

The collection is comprised of material formerly classed as UNCAT WA MS 233, UNCAT MSS 882, UNCAT MSS 1244, UNCAT MSS 1293, UNCAT MSS 1294, UNCAT MSS 1295, UNCAT MSS 1296, UNCAT MSS 1297, UNCAT MSS 1299, UNCAT MSS 1300, UNCAT MSS 1301, UNCAT MSS 1303, and UNCAT MSS 1305.

As a rule, descriptive information found in the Collection Contents section is drawn in large part from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
Two film titles separated in the listing by a slash mark (/) indicates that the two films were promoted together.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Western Film Collection consists of press kits, pressbooks, posters, photographs, souvenir programs and other materials that were distributed by film producers to movie theater operators to promote their films. The Western films represented in this collection are Western genre films, films set in the trans-Mississippi West, and/or films with Western-type characters. Press kits typically include advertisements prepared for submission to newspapers and magazines; black-and-white photographs of scenes from the films with credit information; printed booklets called pressbooks containing information about the film, actors, and other personnel; and posters for lobby display. Early press kits were called campaign kits. While the majority of press kits included in the collection consist of foldered printed material, there are several examples of electronic press kits on compact disk.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Western Film Collection is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

did.unittitle: Zoot Suit, press kit and poster

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a press kit and 25"x38" folder poster for the film from writer/director Luiz Valdez. The film concerns racial tensions against the Latino population in Los Angeles during World War II. It is an early, if not the first, film to consider Chicano and Latino identity in Southern California's history from a Latino perspective.

acqinfo.p: Purchased from Royal Books on the Frederick W. and Carrie S. Beinecke Fund for Western Americana, 2017.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs from the films "Night of the Strangler" and "On Their Own."

did.unittitle: The Ace of Spades (The Night of the Strangler), photos

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains six black and white photos from the film directed by Joy N. Houck Jr. The film was originally released in the United States under the title "The Night of the Strangler," and was reissued under the title "The Ace of Spades." Set in Louisiana, the film concerns a romantic relationship between an African-American man and a caucasian woman, and violence that follows.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains forty black and white set photos from the film directed by Otto Brower. This film was the last in a series of seventeen films released 1936-1940 by 20th Century Fox. The series centers around the "Jones Family," and is a collection of low-budget family comedies. "On Their Own" concerns the Jones Family's move to California after their father becomes ill.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Walter Reuben, Inc. on the William Robertson Coe Fund No. 3 [Western Americana], 2017.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains four blueprints (three dated 1932 and one dated 1936) detailing the Fox studio's physical campus and expansions. Though Fox was in shaky finiancial shape entering the era of silent films, the studio nevertheless grew its physical plant, as talking films necessitated many different sound stages. It is likely that all of these blueprints were printed in 1936 to accompany the most recent one here, as Fox merged with the 20th Century company in 1935, and all of these blueprints bear the post-merger "20th Century Fox" name.
Also contains a folder housing a photocopy of the mailing label from the cylinder that formerly housed the blueprints.

did.unittitle: Any Which Way You Can, photos

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains twenty-nine black and white photos for the film starring Clint Eastwood.

did.unittitle: Bad Day at Black Rock, press kit

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a press kit for the film starring Spencer Tracy. The film concerns the post-war murder of a Japanese-American, and is an unsual Hollywood film of its era in its consideration of racism, especially anti-Asian racism.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a pressbook for the Howard Hawks-directed film starring Kirk Douglas, Dewey Martin, and Elizabeth Threatt. The film is based on a novel by Pulitzer Prize-winning author A.B. Guthrie Jr., and concerns an 1830 journey up the Missouri river.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains thirty-seven photos from the film starring Fred Williamson. The film is set in Venice, California, and is representative of the Blaxploitation film movement.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a large pressbook for the Cecil B. DeMille-produced silent film starring Rod LaRocque and Lillian Rich. The film was adapted from the 1905 play "Strongheart" written by DeMille's brother William, and concerns the then-unusual subject of a Native American tribe dealing with oppression by the white owners of a canning company.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a photo of nine men on the then-new studio lot of Chaplin Studios.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a press kit for the film from writer-director Jim Jarmusch. The film is set, without reference to a specific time, in New Orleans and the surrounding swamps.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains thirty-five black and white photos from the film. This film, an early entry in the New American Cinema movement, was an important expression in the youth counter-culture.

did.unittitle: Fast Times at Ridgemont High, promotional booklet

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a promotional booklet for the film concerning southern California youth culture.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a French pressbook for the silent film starring Buck Jones. This film was released in the United States under the title "Good as Gold."

did.unittitle: Für ein Handvoll Dollar, German photos

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains fourteen color promotional photos for the German-language release of this Sergio Leone-directed film starring Clint Eastwood. The film was released in the United States under the title "A Fistful of Dollars." Though this is typically thought of as an Italian film, it was actually an Italian-West German-Spanish co-production, and many of the supporting actors were German.

did.unittitle: Für ein paar Dollar mehr, German photos

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains twenty-four color German-language promotional photos for this Sergio Leone-directed film starring Clint Eastwood. The film was released in the United States under the title "For a Few Dollars More." Like its predecessor "Für ein Handvoll Dollar/ A Fistful of Dollars," this sequel was an Italian-West German-Spanish co-production. This film is now considered by many a masterpiece, demonstrating more confidently than its predecessor Leone's visual style and Eastwood's anti-hero character. This film was instrumental in establishing Eastwood's American career.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a "film guide" in the form of a "study plan" for students. This guide, released by Paramount Studios, presents a curriculm concerning Native Americans, the American Southwest, and the Apache chief Geronimo, and is an interesting perspective from the era on these topics.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains thirty black and white photos from the film, which concerns California "outlaw" motorcycle culture.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a German souvenir program for the film, a German-language release for the Raoul Walsh-directed epic "The Big Trail." "The Big Trail" was made in an early type of wide screen procress, and was produced before dubbing or subtitling was common practice, and so this version was filmed on the same original set with a different cast. This version of the film may be lost.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains twenty-two photos from the film, which concerns California "outlaw" hot-rod car culture.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains fourteen photos from the film, which concerns California "outlaw" hot-rod car culture.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a pressbook and two supplemental inserts for the Martin Ritt-directed film starring Paul Newman. The film was adapted from a novel by Larry McMurtry, and served as a somewhat shocking deconstruction of the prototypical western hero.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a French pressbook for the silent film starring Buck Jones. This film was released in the United States under the title "The Desert's Price."

did.unittitle: Lakota Woman: Siege at Wounded Knee, photos

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains five black and white photos from the film, which concerns a young Native American woman and her family in South Dakota, and the 1973 protests at Wounded Knee.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains fifteen photos from the film starring Clint Eastwood. This film is the first sequel in the "Dirty Harry" series.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains fifteen photos from the film, which concerns California "outlaw" motorcyle culture.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a pressbook for the film. This pressbook is especially extensive, and details the impressive and extensive advertising campaigns Warner Brothers undertook to promote this film.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a French pressbook for the silent film starring Buck Jones. This film was released in the United States under the title "30 Below Zero."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains twenty-two photos (eight color, fourteen black and white) from the iconic film starring James Dean, Sal Mineo, and Natalie Wood.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains twenty photos (six color, twelve black and white) for the Howard-Hawks directed film starring John Wayne. This film is Hawks' last.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a French pressbook for the silent film starring Buck Jones. This film was released in the United States under the title "Blood Will Tell."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a pressbook for the Raoul Walsh-directed film starring frequent collaborator Errol Flynn, and Ann Sheridan. This film is considered one of Flynn's best, and concerns a gambler who is caught in a conflict between Nevada silver miners.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains thirty-six black and white photos from the Raoul Walsh-directed epic about a cattle drive from Texas to Montana. The film stars Clark Gable and Jane Russell.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a pressbook for the film from writer-director-star Orson Welles. This film is now considered a masterpiece within the noir canon, and also stars Charlton Heston and Janet Leigh.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains twelve color photos from the film, which was directed by and stars Jack Nicholson.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a photo from the entrance to the Warner Brothers campus, and is captioned "Vitaphone Coast #1." The Vitaphone sound system was famously used by Warner Brothers on the "Jazz Singer."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a substantioal promotional book for the Samuel Goldwyn film starring, among others, Gary Cooper, Walter Brennan, Doris Davenport, and Lilian Bond. The book contains a series of production stills mounted and captioned in thick stock pages, and is introduced with a cover letter from the director of advertising and publicity at United Artists. Beginning in 1940, a number of western films more sophisticated than the B-westerns of prior years emerged, and this film, directed by William Wyler, was one such film and a major effort for Samuel Goldwyn.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Walter Reuben, Inc. on the Frederick W. and Carrie S. Beinecke Fund for Western Americana, 2017.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains French pressbooks for 7 Colts du tonnerre [7 magnifiche pistole] (1966); Chapagua [L'oro dei bravados] (1970); El Sartana, l'ombre de ta mort [Passa Sartana... è l'ombra della tua morte] (1968); La vengeance est mon pardon [La Vendetta è il mio perdono] (1968); Le Défi des MacKenna [Un dólar y una tumba / La sfida dei Mackenna] (1970); Le salaire de la haine [Odia il prossimo tuo] (1968); Les Sentiers de la Haine [Il piombo e la carne] (1964); Massacre au Grand Canyon [Massacro al Grande Canyon] (1964); Pour un je tire [T'ammazzo!... Raccomandati a Dio] (1968); Son nom est Sacramento [...e lo chiamarono Spirito Santo] (1971); Trois salopards... une poignée d'or [La più grande rapina del West] (1967).

did.unittitle: German Westerns, pressbooks and ad mats

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains German material related to Andere beten – Django schießt [Se vuoi vivere... spara!] (1968); Bandidos (1967); Der Gehetzte der Sierra Madre [La Resa dei Conti] (1966); Die letzte Kugel traf den Besten [Aventuras del Oeste] (1965); Die letzten Zwei vom Rio Bravo [Le pistole non discutono] (1964); Django - Kreuze im blutigen Sand [Cjamango] (1967); Django und die Bande der Gehenkten [Preparati la bara!] (1968); Django - Die Nacht der langen Messer [Ciakmull - L'uomo della vendetta] (1970); Django, der Rächer [Texas, addio] (1966); Laßt uns töten, Companeros [Vamos a matar, compañeros] (1970); Rocco - der Einzelgänger von Alamo [Ballata per un pistolero] (1967); Rocco - Ich leg' dich um [L'ultimo killer] (1967); Sein Wechselgeld ist Blei [I giorni della violenza] (1967); Stirb oder töte! [Killer calibro 32] (1967); Zorro gegen Maciste [Zorro contro Maciste] (1963).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains German ephemera regarding movies based on Karl May novels. Specifically, Old Shatterhand (1965), Old Surehand (1965), and Winnetou 2.Teil (1964).

did.unittitle: Marching On!, lobby card and photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Film about African American army life during World War Two.

did.unittitle: Paramount Produced Properties and Releases, brad-bound typescript

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains an in-house publication detailing every Paramount film, from the 1912 release of Queen Elizabeth with Sarah Bernhardt.

did.unittitle: The Plainsman and the Lady, drawings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains copies of detailed set drawings, by a draftsman named H. L. Frost. Included are four interiors and one exterior. The set decorators for this film were John McCarthy, Jr. and George Milo.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains material related to The Mine with the Iron Door (1924), Wanderer of the Wasteland (1924), Drifting Thru (1926), Mississippi Gambler (1929), Last Frontier (1932), Silent Enemy (1930), Way Out West (1930), King of the Wild Horses (1933), State Fair (1933), and Eskimo (1934).

did.unittitle: Rogue of the Rio Grande, pressbook

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From the "Exploitation and Press Book" promotional material.

accessrestrict.p: Restricted Fragile Material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
